title: Savastana macrophylla
taxonomy: Savastana macrophylla is classified under the family Poaceae, which is a large and diverse family of grasses. The genus Savastana is a group of grasses that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. More specifically, Savastana macrophylla is believed to be a type of grass that is commonly found in Africa and Asia. It is closely related to other genera such as Bothriochloa and Cenchrus. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification and relationships of this species.
uses: Savastana macrophylla, also known as the Giant Savanna-grass, has been traditionally used in some African cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The leaves and stems of Savastana macrophylla are said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical applications.
